task_completion.description: The agent opened the correct survey document in a word processor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A word processor application (e.g., Calligra Words or LibreOffice Writer) is open with 'blackwood_wetland_survey.odt' visible in the title bar or tab.

task_completion.description: Heading 1 styles are applied to the five main sections.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The section titles 'Introduction', 'Methodology', 'Site Description', 'Flora and Fauna Inventory', and 'Conservation Recommendations' appear significantly larger and bolder than body text, or the style selector shows 'Heading 1' when the cursor is on them.

task_completion.description: Heading 2 styles are applied to the four subsections.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The subsection titles 'Hydrology', 'Soil Composition', 'Canopy Layer', and 'Understory' appear formatted as subheadings (smaller than H1 but distinct from body text), or the style selector shows 'Heading 2'.

task_completion.description: A Table of Contents is inserted immediately following the Introduction.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A structured Table of Contents block listing the document's headings and page numbers is visible right after the 'Introduction' section and before the 'Methodology' section.

task_completion.description: The specified scientific Latin names are italicized in the text.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Instances of the scientific names (Acer rubrum, Typha latifolia, Clemmys guttata, Lithobates catesbeianus, Ardea herodias, Chrysemys picta) are visibly slanted/italicized in the document body.

task_completion.description: The comma-separated observation data at the end of the document is converted into a structured four-column table.
task_completion.visual_evidence: At the end of the document, there is a clear grid/table structure with exactly four columns containing the observation data, replacing the original comma-separated text.

integrity.description: The agent used the word processor's graphical user interface to format the document.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the word processor application open with visible menus, toolbars, and cursor selections, rather than just terminal windows running scripts to modify the .odt file.

integrity.description: The observation data is in a genuine table, not just text aligned with spaces or tabs.
integrity.visual_evidence: The table at the end of the document shows visible cell borders, gridlines, or table-specific UI handles, confirming it is a native table object.

integrity.description: The Table of Contents is a generated element, not manually typed text.
integrity.visual_evidence: The TOC has characteristics of an auto-generated field, such as a gray background highlight when clicked, dotted leader lines, or perfect right-alignment of page numbers.
